title: baccharis rhomboidalis subsp. truncata
intro: Baccharis rhomboidalis subsp. truncata is a plant species, also known as the "Truncata Baccharis" or "Coyote Brush," which is a type of shrub native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Baccharis rhomboidalis subsp. truncata include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, among others. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
